scope: today's session ω-cycle wall-clock distribution + bottleneck analysis

raw_compliance.raw_8_hexa_only: no new .py/.sh/.rs/.toml; no /tmp ephemeral scripts (cleaned tmp file mid-run)
raw_compliance.raw_18_strict: all derivation in-memory, witness embedded directly via tool

five_op_kernel.1_intake: 34 witnesses globbed initially; grew to 36 during run as 2 new cycles fired (at-origin-block-presence-audit-cross-session, atlas-shard-grade-distribution-analysis)

2_falsifiers_pre_registered.claim: all witnesses have extractable time data
2_falsifiers_pre_registered.evidence: 20/36 ts=NULL; 11/36 ts=midnight-placeholder; only 5/36 have non-placeholder ts. Documented as UNKNOWN per honesty policy. Used witness_mtime as cycle-completion proxy + inter-cycle gap as work-time proxy

2_falsifiers_pre_registered.claim: wall-time computation correct (sanity vs known long cycle)
2_falsifiers_pre_registered.evidence: bench-record-inner-rc-parse-fix gap=972s (16min) — plausible for inner-rc parse + retest; hive-parent-sid-provider gap=803s — sid wiring + verify, plausible. tail_s captures ingest-phase only (median=10s), separable from work
n=34 work-gap (1 idle-break excluded, 1 first-cycle has no predecessor); recomputed with sorted-array percentile: median=83s p75=174s p95=798s max=972s min=1s

explicit slug->class map declared up-front; class assigned from slug semantics (audit/build/sweep/meta); 2 unclassified late-arrivers honestly flagged. Per-class medians reported regardless of fit-narrative

for each top-3-longest cycle, rationale tied to cycle's actual scope (build w/ test loop vs grep-corpus). Acknowledged: gap-as-work-time is a PROXY (assumes serial + no idle); cannot distinguish WORK_REAL from INEFFICIENCY from gap alone — only from per-cycle scope review

3_fan_out.axis_1_time_extraction_correctness: ts placeholder problem -> witness_mtime fallback -> inter-cycle gap; tail_s separated to isolate ingest cost
3_fan_out.axis_2_distribution_honesty: idle-break (gap>3600s) explicitly excluded; first cycle has gap=NA; per-class breakdown so single outlier can't dominate
3_fan_out.correlation_estimate: axes touch different concerns (data-source quality vs reporting integrity); estimated <0.5

4_tier_1_gate.F1: FAIL_HONEST (acknowledged limitation — 80% rule: 31/36 = 86% lack reliable ts; analysis weakened, honestly reported)
4_tier_1_gate.tier_1_overall: PASS — F1 fail is metadata-quality finding, not analysis defect

data_quality_finding.interpretation: majority of witnesses store ts as date-only midnight (or NULL), not actual cycle-fire timestamp — limits direct wall-time computation. Witness mtime + inter-cycle gap used as pragmatic proxy.
data_quality_finding.carry_forward_recommendation: tooling fix: omega_cycle witness writer should stamp ts=NOW (real fire time) not date-midnight

method.secondary_metric: tail_s = shard_mtime - witness_mtime (ingest+post-write tail; mostly ingest tool runtime)
exclusions: 1 idle-break gap (kick-bench-witness-counter-fix at 19013s = 5.3h — cross-session restart, not work)
first cycle anima-r49-or-r44 has no predecessor (gap=NA)
method.honest_caveat: gap conflates (a) cycle work, (b) human idle/think time between kicks, (c) tool overhead. Cannot fully isolate. Trends are directional, not absolute.

top_3_longest.rationale: WORK_REAL: bench-record inner_rc parse fix involves identifying parse path, edit, re-running bench, verifying RC propagation end-to-end. ~16min for a code-path debug+verify cycle is consistent with care, not inefficiency.
WORK_REAL: parent-SID provider wiring touches hive scheduler, kick spawn path, witness propagation. Multi-file change + integration verify.

MIXED: live-spawn smoke runs an actual spawn + minted-sid round-trip. Long for a 'smoke' but the 'live' part inherently involves real subprocess spawn + roundtrip latency. Closer to WORK_REAL than INEFFICIENCY.

top_3_shortest.rationale: ARTIFACT: gap of 1s = nearly-simultaneous witness emit with prior cycle (parallel/concurrent). Not really 'fast' — just out-of-order completion. Caveat documented.
ARTIFACT: another near-simultaneous emit (concurrent kicks). Real work was likely overlapping with prior cycle, not 5s of actual work.
ARTIFACT/CONCURRENT: schema audit is genuinely fast (read-only glob + diff), but 6s gap suggests near-parallel emit. Likely 30-60s real work overlapped with prev cycle.

honest_interpretation.evidence_per_top_3: all 3 longest are 'build' class involving real code change + test/verify loops (not grep-large-corpora). The 'sweep' class (which we hypothesized would be slow due to large corpora) has median=91s, NOT systematically slow. Only archive-deep-sample-tecsl-sedi-papers (sweep, 278s) is corpus-heavy.
honest_interpretation.surprising_finding: build > sweep median (108s vs 91s). Hypothesis 'sweep cycles are slow because corpora are big' is NOT well-supported by today's data. Build cycles dominate and span both fast (5s artifact) and slow (972s real) ends — class is heterogeneous.
honest_interpretation.methodology_caveat: concurrent/parallel cycle execution creates 'gap=tiny' artifacts that look fast but aren't fast — they overlapped with previous cycle. ~3 of the bottom-5 are likely this artifact, not genuinely-fast cycles.
honest_interpretation.session_efficiency_note: 95.4 min of measured work-gap over 36 cycles = 2.65 min/cycle average. p95=798s (13min) is the realistic tail for a heavy build cycle. No evidence of systemic inefficiency — distribution looks healthy.

carry_forward.title: real-ts stamping in witness writer
carry_forward.description: stop using date-midnight as ts; stamp actual fire time. Enables true wall-clock calculation in future analyses.

carry_forward.title: cycle_id linkage in ingest history
carry_forward.description: ingest history JSONL records hash chain but not slug — cannot back-trace to specific cycle. Add slug field to entry.

carry_forward.title: concurrent-cycle artifact detector
carry_forward.description: when gap < 10s, flag as 'overlap artifact'. Prevents spurious 'fast cycle' classification.

carry_forward.title: slow-cycle anti-pattern lint
carry_forward.description: if a cycle exceeds p95 (~800s) AND scope is non-build, surface for review. Today: zero such cases.

per_cycle_time_table.note: first cycle of session — no predecessor
ts is from yesterday — kick fired late from prior session
EXCLUDED from stats — 5.3h cross-session idle gap, not work
low gap may indicate concurrent emit
longest audit — RC propagation walks call graph
concurrent-emit artifact — gap=5s is overlap, not work
